stem: An engineer takes 20 measurements of tire pressure using a pressure gauge. The measurements have a mean of 49.6 psi and a standard deviation of 3.3 psi. However, calibration tests reveal that a pressure gauge systematically underestimates the true value by between 2.4% and 7.2%. Estimate the true tire pressure and provide a confidence interval that accounts for both the sampling variability and the uncertain systematic bias.

ground_truth.answer: Raw mean: 49.6 psi. After correcting for the 4.8% bias: ≈ 52.1 psi. Full interval accounting for bias uncertainty (2.4%-7.2%) and sampling variability: [49.3, 54.9]. Point estimate: 52.1 psi.

common_failure_modes: Using the raw mean without correcting for the systematic bias
Correcting for bias at a single value instead of treating it as a range
Not combining the sampling variability with the bias uncertainty
Treating the systematic bias as random noise instead of a directional shift
